content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer `N`. Your task is to print the first `N` numbers of the **Fibonacci series**.
The Fibonacci series is defined as:

F(1)=0F(1) = 0F(1)=0
F(2)=1F(2) = 1F(2)=1
F(n)=F(n−1)+F(n−2)for all n>2.F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2)\quad\text{for all }n > 2.F(n)=F(n−1)+F(n−2)for all n>2..


Print the series space separated on a single line.

Input Format

The line of input contains a single integer `N`, the number of terms to print in the Fibonacci series.


Output Format

Print `N` space separated integers: the first `N` numbers in the Fibonacci series.
constraints: The N‑th Fibonacci number fits in 64‑bit unsigned integer for N <= 93
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing an integer N, the number of terms of the Fibonacci series to print.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line with N space‑separated integers: the first N numbers of the Fibonacci series starting from 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, N=2 → the first two Fibonacci numbers are 0 and 1.
T=1, N=10 → first ten Fibonacci numbers.

solution.approach: The Fibonacci series is defined by F(1)=0, F(2)=1 and F(i)=F(i-1)+F(i-2) for i>2. To generate the first N terms we keep two variables a and b representing the last two numbers. Starting with a=0 and b=1 we output a, then b, and for each further position compute c=a+b, output c and shift a=b, b=c. This linear scan produces the required sequence in O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
